inputs: {"input":"Name one major function of the mitochondria in cells."}
reference_outputs: {"referenceOutput":"A major function of mitochondria is producing ATP (energy) through cellular respiration."}
outputs: {"answer":"The main function of the mitochondria in cells is to carry out cellular respiration, a metabolic process that provides energy for cellular processes such as muscle contraction and neurotransmitter release. Specifically, mitochondria break down fats into fatty acids and glycerol, use oxygen from the air to produce water, and convert electrons from ATP synthase into an electron-carrier system used in other functions of the cell."}
